senses.definition: to carefully consider the facts of a situation in order to understand something, make a decision, or solve a problem

examples.x: You can figure this out if you just use your head.
Come on, use your noodle and find a solution!
Using your head is always better than acting impulsively.

senses.definition: in a very nervous or frustrating state of mind in face of a problem or situation one knows very little or nothing about

examples.x: He's out of his depth in that advanced physics class.
She felt out of her depth when asked to lead the project.
I'm out of my depth with these complicated financial documents.
Entering the world of politics was like being out of his depth for the young candidate.
He quickly realized he was out of his depth in the legal discussion.

word_list.word: your guess is as good as mine

senses.definition: said when one does not have a definite answer to a particular question, similar to the questioner

word_list.word: [get|have] the wrong end of the stick

senses.definition: to fail to understand or interpret or understand something correctly

examples.x: I think you've got the wrong end of the stick.
She got the wrong end of the stick and thought the meeting was canceled.
Don't get the wrong end of the stick; I was just joking.
He had the wrong end of the stick about our plans for the weekend.

word_list.pronunciation: nňĆ…Ďňźt nňąo ä bňąiňźnz …źbňĆa ät ňĆ…õstňĆiňźňąe…™t É

senses.definition: to have no information regarding a particular subject

examples.x: I don't know beans about car engines, so I rely on the mechanic to explain what's wrong.
She doesn't know beans about gardening, so she's taking a gardening class to learn.

senses.definition: used when one is incapable of understanding or remembering a particular thing

examples.x: He couldn't remember the name of the movie for the life of him.
I couldn't find my keys for the life of me.
She couldn't figure out how to use the software for the life of her.
They couldn't locate the source of the noise for the life of them.
We couldn't understand the math problem for the life of us.

senses.definition: someone or something one knows very little or nothing about

examples.x: The workings of the stock market are a closed book to me ‚Äď I don't understand how it works.
The new technology is a closed book to most people ‚Äď they're not familiar with it yet.
My new neighbor is a closed book to me ‚Äď I don't know anything about them.
The mysterious stranger was a closed book to everyone in the village ‚Äď no one knew who he was or where he came from.
The reclusive scientist was a closed book to his colleagues ‚Äď he rarely interacted with them and never shared his work.

senses.definition: used to describe something that is complicated due to not being clearly described or shown

examples.x: His instructions for assembling the furniture were clear as mud; I had to figure it out on my own.
The professor's lecture on quantum physics was as clear as mud to the non-science majors in the class.
I tried reading the legal contract, but the language used in it was as clear as mud.

senses.definition: to be incapable of understanding even the simplest concepts

examples.x: I asked him to fix the computer, but he doesn't know his ass from his elbow when it comes to technology.
Don't ask him for directions; he's hopeless with maps and doesn't know his ass from his elbow when it comes to navigating.
The new intern doesn't know his ass from his elbow when it comes to office procedures.
When it comes to technology, he doesn't know his ass from his elbow.

senses.definition: the limits of human knowledge or understanding, representing areas that remain unknown or unexplored

examples.x: For scientists, the nature of consciousness remains the final frontier of human understanding.
Exploring the depths of the ocean presents the final frontier for marine biologists.
In philosophy, the nature of existence and consciousness is often considered the final frontier.
Quantum mechanics represents the final frontier of physics, challenging our understanding of the fundamental nature of reality.
Understanding the origins of the universe and the fundamental laws of physics remains the final frontier of scientific inquiry.

senses.definition: confused and unable to think clearly

examples.x: I've been so busy lately that I'm in a fog when it comes to my schedule.
After the accident, he was in a fog and couldn't remember what happened.
The sudden news left her in a fog of disbelief and confusion.

senses.definition: to fully understand something difficult, complicated, or strange

examples.x: Sam tries to wrap her brain around snow and freezing temperatures, but she's always lived in Florida, so real winter is not something she has experienced.

senses.definition: to make an attempt to understand someone or something that is puzzling

examples.x: The detective couldn't make head or tail out of the crime scene evidence.
The detective was trying to make head or tail of the crime scene.
